basic_details.job_title: UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for Assistant Professor, Scientific Officer and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Assistant Professor, Scientific Officer and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) Notification 2026 has been released on the official website upsc.gov.in. A total of 5 vacancies have been announced through the UPSC Recruitment 2026 for Assistant Professor, Scientific Officer and More Posts.

age_limit.age_details: Standard UPSC relaxations apply. Specific limits not provided.
age_limit.details: All Posts: Standard UPSC relaxations apply. Specific age limits are not detailed in the provided data. Age relaxation details: OBC: +3 years; SC/ST: +5 years; PwBD: +10 years (max age not exceed 56 years); PwBD+OBC: +13 years; PwBD+SC/ST: +15 years; Central Govt. employees: +5 years; Ex-Servicemen: +5 years.

qualification.education_qualification: Master’s Degree in Physics, B.E./B.Tech in Mechanical/Metallurgical Engineering, B.E./B.Tech in Electrical/Mechanical/Metallurgy, Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering, B.E./B.Tech in Civil Engineering + M.E./M.Tech in Civil Engineering (Geo Informatics / Geoinformatics and Surveying Technology)
qualification.details: Scientist-B (Mechanical): A) Master’s Degree in Physics + 1 year practical experience in testing/calibration of engineering materials/products or precision measurements/metrology OR B) B.E./B.Tech in Mechanical/Metallurgical Engineering + 2 years practical experience in testing/calibration of engineering materials/products or precision measurements/metrology (in recognized lab/institute). Desirable: R&D experience in testing of engineering materials and metrology/calibration. Scientific Officer (Non-Destructive): Master’s Degree in Physics OR B.E./B.Tech in Electrical/Mechanical/Metallurgy + 1 year experience in Non-Destructive testing Laboratory OR 1 year experience in X-Ray Diffraction or investigation of failure of Engineering Material by Non-Destructive and metallographic methods. Superintending Archaeological Engineer: Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering + Eight years’ experience in Planning, Execution and Supervision of Civil Engineering works in Government Sector or Archaeological/Heritage Sites. Assistant Professor, Civil Engineering (Survey): B.E./B.Tech in Civil Engineering + M.E./M.Tech in Civil Engineering (Geo Informatics / Geoinformatics and Surveying Technology) with first class or equivalent either at Bachelor’s or Master’s level.

salary_details.pay_scale: Level 08-11 & Academic Level-10 in Pay Matrix as per 7th CPC
salary_details.details: Scientist-B (Mechanical): Level-10 in the Pay Matrix as per 7th CPC Scientific Officer (Non-Destructive): Level-08 in the Pay Matrix as per 7th CPC Superintending Archaeological Engineer: Level-11 in the Pay Matrix as per 7th CPC Assistant Professor, Civil Engineering (Survey): Academic Level-10 in the Pay Matrix as per 7th CPC

selection_process: desirable qualifications Recruitment Test (if decided by UPSC) Interview (Personality Test)
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online on or before 15-05-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of UPSC to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the UPSC recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application form for the UPSC recruitment is 15th May 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in this UPSC recruitment?
faq.answer: There are a total of 5 vacancies announced by the Union Public Service Commission for various posts.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for UPSC recruitment?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 25/- for General/OBC male candidates. Female candidates, SC, and ST candidates are exempted from paying the application fee.

faq.question: Q4. What are the minimum qualifications required for the posts?
faq.answer: The minimum qualifications vary by post, including Master's degrees in Physics, Bachelor's/Master's in Engineering disciplines like Mechanical, Civil, and Metallurgy.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary structure for the selected candidates?
faq.answer: The salary ranges from Level 08 to Level 11 in the Pay Matrix as per the 7th CPC, with Academic Level-10 for the Assistant Professor post.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for UPSC recruitment?
faq.answer: The selection process typically involves shortlisting based on qualifications and experience, followed by a Recruitment Test (if decided by UPSC) and an Interview.

faq.question: Q7. Can candidates from outside West Bengal apply?
faq.answer: The provided data mentions "Kolkata, West Bengal" as the state, but the application is online. Candidates should refer to the official notification for specific eligibility regarding location.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any age relaxation for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Yes, age relaxation is provided for OBC (+3 years), SC/ST (+5 years), and PwBD (+10 years), with further relaxations for combined categories.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I find the official notification PDF?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF can be accessed through the "Important Links" section of this article, labeled as "Official Notification PDF".

faq.question: Q10. How do I apply online for the UPSC recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online by visiting the official UPSC website or through the direct "Apply Online" link provided in the "Important Links" section before the closing date.
